LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HAWK LEE MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS WHICH SUPPORT A REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION. Off Calendar Jul-06-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HAWK LEE MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted with fifteen days leave to amend. The first amended complaint does not allege ultimate facts which support a request for punitive damages. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, ultimate facts showing that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
